title: 中考六选五 10: Group Work Is Not Always Teamwork
passage:
Group work is common in school. Teachers ask students to prepare posters, do experiments or make presentations together. 1. ______ But simply putting students into a group does not always create real teamwork.

One problem is that some students do most of the work while others do very little. This may happen because the active students want a good result quickly. 2. ______ In the end, the group finishes the task, but not everyone learns.

Another problem is unclear roles. If nobody knows who should collect information, design slides or speak in front of the class, time may be wasted. 3. ______ Clear roles make the work fairer and more efficient.

Good teamwork also needs communication. Students should not only divide tasks, but also check whether their parts fit together. 4. ______ Otherwise, the final work may look like several separate pieces put together carelessly.

After the project, it is useful for the group to reflect. They can ask what went well and what should be improved next time. 5. ______ This makes group work a real learning experience, not just a shared assignment.

questions.label: Questions 1-5 - Sentence Insertion
questions.instruction: Choose the best sentence from A-F for each blank in the passage. There is one extra sentence you do not need.

choices.text: As a result, quieter students may not get the chance to practise.
This can help students learn from both success and problems.
In theory, group work can help students learn cooperation.
A short discussion before starting can prevent confusion later.
They need to make sure the whole project has one clear direction.
The best group is always the one with the highest score.
